judgment.summary: Rapidly growing company with strong revenue expansion and a significant earnings beat, but trades at a very high multiple and shows low profitability and high leverage.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimate by 242.4%; next earnings date is 2026-04-28.
positives: Revenue growth 130.4% YoY — exceptional top-line expansion.
Last quarter EPS surprise 242.4% — significant beat on earnings.
Analyst growth estimates are very high for current Q (286.1%) and FY (179.5%).
Strong cash position ($2.49B) and healthy current (5.03) and quick (3.98) ratios.
Consensus analyst rating is 'buy' with a high mean target.
negatives: Forward P/E of 73.08x and Price/Sales of 35.62x are extremely high.
Profit margin is very low at 0.2%, indicating thin profitability.
Return on equity is only 1.3%, suggesting inefficient use of shareholder capital.
Debt/Equity ratio of 311.48 is very high, indicating significant leverage.
Mean analyst target ($245.13) is below the current spot price ($305.77).

news.paragraph: Bloom Energy (BE) is being highlighted as a potential top climate change stock, with recent analyst reports discussing its position in the sector. The company's recent earnings report showed a significant EPS beat, far exceeding analyst expectations for the last quarter. While the stock is receiving positive analyst attention, the upcoming earnings date should be monitored for any further guidance or performance updates.

headlines.title: Is Bloom Energy (BE) One of the Best Climate Change Stocks to Buy According to Analysts?
